Question: Let's build your treatment plan
Description: This takes about 3 minutes. We'll ask about your health goals, medical history, and match you with the right treatments.

Question: What are your health goals?
Description: Select all that apply — this helps us personalize your visit.
Answer Options: Energy & vitality / Sexual performance / Hair growth / Muscle & strength / Weight management / Sleep quality / Anti-aging & longevity / General wellness

Question: What sex were you assigned at birth?
Answer Options: Male / Female

Question: What is your age?
Triggers: If basics-age = "0-18" → DISQUALIFY __all__: "We are unable to provide treatment to patients under 18. Please consult with your primary care physician."

Question: Are you currently pregnant or breastfeeding?
Answer Options: Yes / No
Triggers: If basics-pregnant = "yes" → DISQUALIFY __peptides__: "Peptide treatments are not available during pregnancy or while breastfeeding."

Question: What state do you live in?
Answer Options: Alabama / Alaska / Arizona / Arkansas / California / Colorado / Connecticut / Delaware / Florida / Georgia / Hawaii / Idaho / Illinois / Indiana / Iowa / Kansas / Kentucky / Louisiana / Maine / Maryland / Massachusetts / Michigan / Minnesota / Mississippi / Missouri / Montana / Nebraska / Nevada / New Hampshire / New Jersey / New Mexico / New York / North Carolina / North Dakota / Ohio / Oklahoma / Oregon / Pennsylvania / Rhode Island / South Carolina / South Dakota / Tennessee / Texas / Utah / Vermont / Virginia / Washington / West Virginia / Wisconsin / Wyoming / District of Columbia

Question: Are you currently taking any medications, vitamins, or dietary supplements?
Answer Options: Yes / No, I don't take anything
Follow-Up: If med-medications = "yes" → "Please list all medications, vitamins, and supplements including dosage and reason."

Question: Do you have any allergies or sensitivities to any food, dyes, medicines, herbs, vitamins, or supplements?
Answer Options: Yes / Not that I am aware of

Question: Do you have any medical conditions?
Answer Options: Yes / No
Follow-Up: If med-conditions = "yes" → "Please list your medical conditions."

Question: Have you had any surgeries?
Answer Options: Yes / No

Question: When was the last time you had an in-person medical evaluation, including lab tests?
Answer Options: Less than 1 year ago / 1–2 years ago / More than 2 years ago
Triggers: If med-physical-exam = "more-2yr" AND oral-sermorelin is in treatment plan → DISQUALIFY oral-sermorelin: "Sermorelin requires an in-person medical evaluation within the last 2 years."

Question: Have you had your blood pressure measured in the past 6 months?
Answer Options: Yes / No
Triggers: If cardio-bp-recent = "no" → DISQUALIFY ed-rapid-dissolve-tablet: "A recent blood pressure reading is required before prescribing ED medications. Please have your blood pressure measured and try again."

Question: What is your systolic blood pressure number? (The top number)
What is your diastolic blood pressure number? (The bottom number)
Do you have any significant cardiovascular risks?
Answer Options: Cardiac death or heart attack in a first degree relative before the age of 55 / Elevated blood pressure / Diabetes / Elevated cholesterol / Previous history of a heart attack / None apply to me

Question: Do you have any of these heart or vascular conditions?
Description: On occasion these conditions are high risk and it is better that your condition is evaluated in person.
Answer Options: Heart attack, heart failure, or narrowing of heart arteries / Irregular heartbeat or heart arrhythmias / Heart abnormalities or heart murmurs / Long QT syndrome or a prolonged QT interval / Heart valve problems / Cardiomyopathy or enlarged heart / Low blood pressure / Syncope or history of passing out / Pulmonary hypertension / None apply

Question: Do you experience any of the following?
Answer Options: Exertional chest pain (angina) or shortness of breath / Unexplained fainting, lightheadedness, or dizziness / Abnormal heartbeat / Leg cramps with exercise / None apply

Question: How confident are you in getting/keeping an erection?
Answer Options: Not confident / Low confidence / Somewhat confident / Mostly confident / Very confident

Question: How difficult is it to keep an erection to the finish of intercourse?
Answer Options: Extremely difficult / Very difficult / Somewhat difficult / Slightly difficult / Not difficult
Triggers: If ed-difficulty = "extremely" OR ed-difficulty = "very" OR ed-difficulty = "somewhat" → Suggest ed-rapid-dissolve-tablet: "Based on your symptoms, your provider may recommend this combination ED treatment."

Question: What symptoms are you currently experiencing?
Answer Options: Difficult to get an erection / Erection doesn't stay hard during sex / Erection is inconsistent / Ejaculate too quickly or prematurely (PE) / Other
Follow-Up: If ed-symptoms includes "other" → "Please describe your symptoms."

Question: How long have you noticed these symptoms?
Answer Options: Less than 1 month / 2–6 months / 6–12 months / Over 12 months

Question: Have you been treated for ED in the past or tried any medications for erectile dysfunction?
Answer Options: Yes / No
Follow-Up: If ed-previous-treatment = "yes" → "Please list all medications, surgical implants, or penile injections used."

Question: Have you been seen by a healthcare provider for a physical exam in the last 2 years that included a genital exam?
Answer Options: Yes / No
Triggers: If ed-genital-exam = "no" → DISQUALIFY enclomiphene: "Enclomiphene requires a physical exam including genital exam within the last 2 years."

Question: Were there any abnormalities during that exam?
Answer Options: Yes, abnormalities were found / No, my exam was normal
Follow-Up: If ed-genital-abnormalities = "yes" → "Please describe the abnormalities found."

Question: Do you have any of these conditions related to sexual function?
Answer Options: Peyronie's Disease (a marked curve or bend in the penis) / Tight foreskin or foreskin that is hard to retract / Painful ejaculation / Priapism (painful erection lasting more than 4 hours) / Any other deformity or penile deformity / None apply to me

Question: Are you taking any of the following medications?
Description: These medications can cause serious, life-threatening complications when combined with ED treatments.
Answer Options: Nitrates (Nitroglycerin, Isosorbide mononitrate/dinitrate, Nitroprusside) / Sildenafil (Revatio) or any other medication used to treat Pulmonary Hypertension / Alpha-Blockers (Doxazosin, Prazosin, Terazosin, Phentolamine) / HIV medication (protease inhibitors) / Ketoconazole or itraconazole (Nizoral or Sporanox) / Erythromycin or clarithromycin / I don't take any of these medications

Question: Do you use any of these recreational drugs?
Description: Certain recreational drugs can cause life-threatening interactions with ED medications.
Answer Options: Marijuana / Cocaine or crack / Amyl Nitrate, butyl nitrite, Poppers, or Rush / Ecstasy (Molly, MDMA) / I do not use any recreational drugs
Triggers: If ed-recreational-drugs includes "amyl-nitrate" → DISQUALIFY ed-rapid-dissolve-tablet: "Amyl nitrate/poppers use is a contraindication for ED medications due to dangerous blood pressure interactions."

Question: How many alcoholic beverages do you consume per week?
Description: Alcohol should be limited while using ED medication due to potential drops in blood pressure.

Question: Can you easily walk up 2 flights of stairs or walk 20 blocks on flat ground?
Answer Options: Yes / No

Question: How would you rate your energy level?
Answer Options: Terrible / Poor / Average / Good / Excellent
Triggers: If test-energy = "terrible" OR test-energy = "poor" → Suggest enclomiphene: "Your energy responses suggest your provider may want to evaluate your testosterone levels."

Question: How would you rate your muscle strength/endurance?
Answer Options: Terrible / Poor / Average / Good / Excellent

Question: How would you rate your libido (sex drive)?
Answer Options: Terrible / Poor / Average / Good / Excellent

Question: How strong are your erections?
Answer Options: Extremely weak / Weak / Neither weak nor strong / Strong / Extremely strong
Triggers: If test-erections = "extremely-weak" OR test-erections = "weak" OR test-erections = "neutral" → Suggest ed-rapid-dissolve-tablet: "Low erection strength combined with ED symptoms may benefit from a combination ED treatment."

Question: Have you been treated for low testosterone in the past or tried any prescription and/or over-the-counter medications for low testosterone?
Answer Options: Yes / No
Follow-Up: If test-previous-treatment = "yes" → "Please list all medications and products used."

Question: Are you starting Enclomiphene for the first time, or refilling an existing prescription?
Answer Options: Starting for the first time / Refilling (I have been using the medication)

Answer Options: Azelaic Acid / Finasteride / Tretinoin / Hydrocortisone / Minoxidil / Triamcinolone / Ketoconazole / Dutasteride / Caffeine / Latanoprost or Bimatoprost / Melatonin / Other / None of these medications

Description: 5-alpha-reductase inhibitors (finasteride/dutasteride) may worsen depression or anxiety. If you have a history of these conditions, discuss with your provider. If you experience thoughts of self-harm, stop the medication immediately and call 911.
Answer Options: Yes, I acknowledge and want to proceed / No
Triggers: If hair-5ar-warning = "no" → DISQUALIFY __hair__: "Acknowledgment of the 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor warning is required for hair loss treatments."
